Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey,CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP appeared for plaintiffs.
Wrongful Death Heirs Jess Ford, Dondi Visser and Darrylynn Ford were placed under oath and testified.
All three Wrongful Death Heirs appeared via CourtCall for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by Wrongful Death Heir Darlene McCrystal.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds negotiated after the September 11, 2018 death of Darryl Ford in the following proportions:

Estate of Darryl Ford, Deceased - 0%
Darlene McCrystal, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
Jess Ford, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
Dondi Visser, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
Darrylynn Ford, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the September 11, 2018 death of Henry Beasley shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Darlene McCrystal shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate and wrongful death claims with defendants in all claims with settlements negotiated after Darryl Ford's death.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on April 29, 2014 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake.
